summary: This week's AI news highlights significant advancements in AI technology, ethics, and regulation. Major companies like Meta, Apple, and Google are investing heavily in AI, while also facing lawsuits and criticism over their use of AI. The US military has deployed AI-powered drone boats, and New York has become the first state to halt construction of new data centers.
insight: The increasing use of AI in various industries is raising concerns about job displacement, bias, and accountability, highlighting the need for more transparent and regulated AI development and deployment.
highlights: Meta faces lawsuit over alleged use of AI in layoff decisions
Apple releases iOS 27 public beta with revamped Siri AI
Google announces changes to image search with more AI-powered features

articles.title: Lawsuit claims Meta's layoff decisions were made by AI, not humans
articles.description: Meta denies using AI to terminate workers with disabilities and medical problems.

articles.title: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta

articles.title: Google faces another AI training lawsuit from major publishers
articles.description: Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, and other publishers allege that Google trained its AI on copyrighted works without the necessary permissions.

articles.title: DeepMind CEO calls for an independent standards body to regulate frontier AI
articles.description: DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis is proposing an AI "standards body" modeled after FINRA, to test frontier models and develop best practices for their release.

articles.title: Meta accused of using biased AI targeting for mass layoffs

articles.title: Apple sues OpenAI after ex-engineer allegedly used bug to steal trade secrets
articles.description: OpenAI accused of conspiring with former Apple employees to steal trade secrets.
